name: align_exceeds_input
code:
proof fn phi_1_align_exceeds_input(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) > a,
{
}
reason: Downward alignment must never produce a value exceeding the original input

name: align_identity_when_unaligned
code:
proof fn phi_2_align_identity_when_unaligned(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a % (b as int) != 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) == a,
{
}
reason: When the input is not already aligned, align_to_usize must actually change the value

name: align_result_not_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_3_align_result_not_aligned(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        !aligned(align_to_usize(a, b) as nat, b as nat),
{
}
reason: The whole purpose of align_to_usize is to produce a b-aligned result; failing that is a spec bug

name: aligned_implies_zero
code:
proof fn phi_4_aligned_implies_zero(addr: nat, size: nat)
    requires
        size > 0,
        aligned(addr, size),
    ensures
        addr == 0nat,
{
}
reason: Many non-zero addresses are validly aligned; collapsing aligned to only zero would break all page-table reasoning

name: align_always_zero
code:
proof fn phi_5_align_always_zero(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a >= b,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) == 0usize,
{
}
reason: For inputs at least as large as the alignment, the result must be nonzero; always-zero would be a catastrophic spec error
